name: DeepChem (Deep Learning for Drug Discovery)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Bharath Ramsundar et al.

performance.notes: PyTorch/TF backends; GPU training; molecular featurization is CPU-bound

agent_readiness.notes: ML/DL for molecular property prediction, drug discovery, materials science. import deepchem as dc. tasks, datasets, transformers = dc.molnet.load_tox21(). model = dc.models.AttentiveFPModel(n_tasks=12). model.fit(datasets[0]). Featurizers: CircularFingerprint, MolGraphConvFeaturizer, CoulombMatrix. Models: AttentiveFP, GCN, MPNN, SchNet, ChemBERTa. Datasets: MoleculeNet (ADMET, toxicity, binding affinity). Splits: scaffold, fingerprint. MoleculeNet benchmark suite. Sequence: protein/DNA featurization. Active learning. Integrated with RDKit and PyG.

gotchas: API changes frequently between minor versions
MoleculeNet benchmark datasets require download on first use
best_when: Molecular property prediction with GNNs (AttentiveFP, MPNN)
Drug discovery benchmarking on MoleculeNet datasets
When building on top of established cheminformatics ML architectures
Research requiring end-to-end SMILES → property prediction
avoid_when: Traditional molecular fingerprint ML (RDKit + sklearn simpler)
When PyTorch Geometric + RDKit provides more control
summary: DeepChem provides deep learning for drug discovery. dc.molnet.load_tox21() loads benchmark datasets. AttentiveFPModel, GCN, MPNN for molecular property prediction. MolGraphConvFeaturizer converts SMILES to graph features. MoleculeNet benchmarks (ADMET, toxicity, binding). Scaffold splits for proper ML evaluation. Integrates RDKit and PyTorch Geometric. Heavy dependency stack. Best for research using established GNN architectures for molecular ML.
